name: Yacimiento Celtibérico, Castillo de Cervera – circular por GR93
region_intro: Mountain biking around Aguilar Del Río Alhama offers diverse landscapes in the foothills of the Iberian System, traversed by the Alhama River. The region features low-altitude mountainous terrain, providing a scenic backdrop for various routes. Riders can expect a mix of river valleys, rocky crags, and extensive holm oak groves. This area provides varied terrain for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Aguilar Del Río Alhama The most popular mountain bike trail is Cañón del Val, Los Fayos y Vozmediano – circular desde Ágreda, a 24.0 miles (38.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 30 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular journey through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Yacimiento Celtibérico, Castillo de Cervera – circular por GR93, a difficult 21.0 miles (33.8 km) path. This route includes historical points of interest like the Celtiberian archaeological site and Cervera Castle. Local mountain bikers also love the Tarazona – Castillo de Vozmediano loop from Ágreda, a 33.3 miles (53.5 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 9 minutes. Mountain biking around Aguilar Del Río Alhama is defined by the Alhama River Valley, low-altitude mountainous terrain, and holm oak groves. The network offers options for moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Aguilar Del Río Alhama are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 60 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Aguilar Del Río Alhama's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Aguilar Del Río Alhama?
A: There are currently 15 mountain bike trails listed around Aguilar Del Río Alhama on komoot. These routes offer a variety of experiences, from moderate to difficult, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain bike trails in this area?
A: The mountain bike trails around Aguilar Del Río Alhama are primarily categorized as moderate and difficult. There are 8 moderate routes and 7 difficult routes, offering challenges for experienced riders and rewarding journeys for those seeking a good workout.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes in Aguilar Del Río Alhama?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in the area are circular. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058249541/canon-del-val-los-fayos-y-vozmediano-circular-desde-agreda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón del Val, Los Fayos y Vozmediano – circular desde Ágreda&lt;/a&gt;, or the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993872443/yacimiento-celtiberico-castillo-de-cervera-circular-por-gr93" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yacimiento Celtibérico, Castillo de Cervera – circular por GR93&lt;/a&gt;, which incorporates historical sites.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while mountain biking near Aguilar Del Río Alhama?
A: The region offers diverse natural beauty. You'll encounter the lush Alhama River Valley, dramatic rocky crags, and extensive holm oak groves on Monegro Mountain. Unique paleontological attractions include the oldest dinosaur footprints in La Rioja. You might also pass by natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3598457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Roscas&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de las Truchas Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks accessible by mountain bike?
A: Absolutely. The area is rich in history. You can cycle past the significant Celtiberian archaeological site of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338604" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Contrebia Leucade&lt;/a&gt;, or explore routes that include ancient castles and Romanesque hermitages like Santa María de La Antigua. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993872443/yacimiento-celtiberico-castillo-de-cervera-circular-por-gr93" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yacimiento Celtibérico, Castillo de Cervera – circular por GR93&lt;/a&gt; route specifically highlights some of these historical points.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Aguilar Del Río Alhama?
A: The komoot community rates the mountain bike trails in Aguilar Del Río Alhama highly, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Riders often praise the varied terrain, which includes river valleys, rocky formations, and holm oak groves, providing a scenic and engaging experience.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking longer distances, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40315754" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Queiles River Trail loop from Estación de Ágreda&lt;/a&gt; cover over 53 km with significant elevation gain, offering a challenging and rewarding ride. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40314471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarazona – Castillo de Vozmediano loop from Ágreda&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 53 km long.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that pass through charming villages?
A: Yes, the Alhama Greenway, a notable accessible MTB trail in the region, connects various points of interest and passes through charming villages like Inestrillas, offering a blend of natural scenery and local culture.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: The terrain varies from the relatively flat and accessible paths of the Alhama Greenway, which follows the river, to more challenging low-altitude mountainous terrain with significant elevation changes. You'll encounter a mix of riverbanks, rocky sections, and paths through holm oak forests.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region offers several viewpoints. Monegro Mountain, known as the 'roof of Aguilar,' provides panoramic views. You can also find specific viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Tío Fresquito&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic vistas along some routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Aguilar Del Río Alhama?
A: While specific seasonal considerations are not detailed, the region's low-altitude mountainous terrain suggests that spring and autumn would offer pleasant temperatures for mountain biking. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring colder conditions, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any trails that feature water bodies like lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, some routes may lead you past or near water bodies. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5575390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Val Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a notable feature in the area that could be incorporated into a ride, offering a scenic break.
tips: This is one of the best views of the GR 93. Coming from Cornago, you reach Cervera del Río Alhama via a cliff where there are some ruins that reveal the Alhama valley. On the ridge above Cervera are the ruins of a typical Moorish fortress built on the most inaccessible site.

This section is located in the wedge of a ravine between two slopes. The water of the ravine has eroded the clay and limestone for thousands of years, leaving walls and cliffs with projections where vultures nest, which can be seen, flying or perched, majestically.

Remains of the oldest population settlements in La Rioja, located on the right bank of the Alhama river.

The pelendones, a Celtiberian people, settled in Contrebia Leukade in the Iron Age and built a fortification, urbanizing the land and building their houses carved into the rock.

Later, during the second half of the 4th century B.C. Different tribes arrived that expanded the town and reinforced it with a deep ditch dug into the rock and a wall that surrounded the entire city.

This spectacular archaeological site can be toured with a guided tour. It is mandatory to book by phone (941 19 71 19) or by email (contrebialeucade@gmail.com).

waypoints.name: Views of the Castle of Cervera from the GR 93
Celtiberian Archaeological Site of Contrebia Leucade
